title: Add HIERPARAM pretend error code
body:
Can we roll back the changes from #6456 until we can do that check while still allowing constant elaboration of interface parameters?  There was some baby in that bathwater.

This PR reverts b794c7c8d44c8ad4cc39b996e3a8f2f0872ee7fe and 7f85d7f45386993e942dd201bb8ec4cf382ffa29.

I agree that the scenario shown in #6456 should not be allowed, but we (and I presume others) have built a lot on constant access to interface parameters in the past ten years:
https://github.com/verilator/verilator/issues/996

That discussion (from the before-GitHub times) revolved around getter methods, but at some point (I'd have to dig around some more) we decided to change course and do dotted access instead.  The three ways I've seen this done are:

- constant getter method
- dotted access
- $bits() on a signal that is the width of the (presumably numeric) parameter you want access to

The last one is clearly shameful and while I used to have a breakdown of tools on this, I believe it's true that most are going the dotted route.  I know for sure that slang, xcelium, genus, vivado and (until recently) verilator all work with the dotted mechanism.
